docs.title: Quickstart
docs.text: Our example notebook shows how you can download a dataset from heiData.

docs.title: Online
docs.text:
You can run our example notebook online without installing anything on your computer.

  • Google Colab

    Open In Colab

    (Google account required)

  • Binder

    Open in Binder

docs.title: Locally
docs.text:
Alternatively you can run it on your computer.

First clone the repository using git:

git clone https://github.com/ssciwr/QHCC
cd QHCC

Then install the required Python libraries and launch JupyterLab:

pipcondamicromamba 
pip install -r requirements.txt
jupyter lab notebooks/example.ipynb

conda env create -f environment.yml
conda activate QHCC
jupyter lab notebooks/example.ipynb

micromamba env create -f environment.yml
micromamba activate QHCC
jupyter lab notebooks/example.ipynb

docs.title: Continuous Integration
docs.text: Every commit to this repository is tested using continuous integration (CI). This ensures that the code is always working and that new changes do not break existing functionality.

docs.title: GitHub Actions
docs.text:
The CI is implemented using GitHub Actions. The example notebooks and the example scripts from github.com/ZhaoWenzhao/QHCC are run in the CI to ensure that they are working as expected, on all supported platforms.

This is done for different Python versions (3.8, 3.9), operating systems (Linux, Windows, Mac) and dependency managers (pip and conda):

docs.title: Website
docs.text: This website is also built and deployed using mkdocs as part of this CI process.

docs.title: heiData
docs.text:
The datasets will be stored on heiData which also provides a DOI.

Here is an example dataset: doi:10.11588/data/TKCFEF

One way to download this dataset is to go to the link, click "Access Dataset", then "Download ZIP".

However if you are then going to use the data from a Python script or jupyter notebook then consider using the Pooch library instead.

docs.title: Pooch
docs.text:
Pooch is a Python library for downloading datasets, and it has native support for heiData. To install:

pipconda 
pip install pooch

conda install pooch

To download the example dataset from heiData using Pooch:

from pooch import DOIDownloader

downloader = DOIDownloader()
downloader("doi:10.11588/data/TKCFEF/tiny-data.txt", output_file="tiny_data.txt", pooch=None)

docs.title: Models
docs.text:
Example models and data are provided at github.com/ZhaoWenzhao/QHCC.

In order to run the provided example scripts, you will need to use Python 3.8 or 3.9, and install the required Python libraries.

docs.title: pip & requirements.txt
docs.text:
You can do this with pip, by first downloading requirements.txt and then running:

pip 
pip install -r requirements.txt

docs.title: conda & environment.yml
docs.text:
You could also use conda or micromamba, by first downloading environment.yml and then running:

condamicromamba 
conda env create -f environment.yml
conda activate QHCC

micromamba env create -f environment.yml
micromamba activate QHCC

docs.title: direct pip/conda
docs.text:
Alternatively you can install the required Python libraries directly:

pipcondamicromamba 
pip install jupyter numpy pandas pooch pyradiomics simpleitk scikit-learn xgboost

conda env create -n QHCC jupyter numpy pandas pooch python radiomics::pyradiomics simpleitk scikit-learn xgboost -c conda-forge -c radiomics
conda activate QHCC

micromamba env create -n QHCC jupyter numpy pandas pooch python radiomics::pyradiomics simpleitk scikit-learn xgboost -c conda-forge -c radiomics
micromamba activate QHCC
